title: Fermented Crispy Japanese Samphire Gyoza
description: Hangover-cure Japanese dish made with fermented vegetables, crispy samphire, and lacquer glaze, served in a donabe.
instructions: Mix fermented daikon, cabbage, ginger, and garlic in a bowl.
Add soy sauce, sake, sugar, and rice vinegar to the bowl and mix well.
Add cornstarch to the bowl and mix until the mixture is smooth.
Heat donabe over medium heat and add water to the bottom of the donabe.
Place the mixture on the donabe and steam for 5 minutes.
Remove the donabe from heat and let it cool down.
Roll out the gyoza wrappers and place a tablespoon of the mixture in the center of each wrapper.
Fold the wrapper over the filling and press the edges together to seal the gyoza.
Heat a pan over medium heat and add a small amount of oil.
Add the gyoza to the pan and cook until the bottom is golden brown.
Flip the gyoza over and cook until the other side is golden brown.
Brush the lacquer glaze over the gyoza and serve hot.
